hypothesis: A canonical placeholder hypothesis used by ref-form experiments that share this design.

null_model.description: Row-permuted surrogate under the same observer projection.
null_model.generating_process: Permute response rows after the observer projection is fixed.
null_model.independence_argument: Permutation acts on the response axis only; the (b,e) mapping of the design matrix is preserved, so the null inherits no structural coupling from the clustered positive arm.

decision_rules.accept_criterion: Observed test statistic exceeds 99th percentile of the null distribution across >=80% of trials.
decision_rules.reject_criterion: Observed test statistic within 95th percentile of null in a majority of trials.

observer_projection.description: Canonical QA primitives (b,e) with derived (d=b+e, a=b+2e).

source_mapping.mapping_rationale: The canonical protocol is a shared design contract for null independence, pre-registration, decision rules, and observer projection.

ablation.destroyed_structure: randomize the QA observer labels while preserving the input data and decision rule
ablation.expected_direction: claim metric should drop toward the null distribution
